title: One Night in Miami...
logline: On the night Cassius Clay defeats Sonny Liston to become heavyweight champion, he celebrates with three friends - Malcolm X, Sam Cooke, and Jim Brown - in a Miami motel room where their conversations reveal deep tensions about fame, activism, and their roles in the civil rights movement.
antagonistOrConflict: Internal conflicts about identity, purpose, and responsibility within the context of systemic racism and the civil rights movement
tone: Intimate, contemplative, and charged with underlying tension
themes: The responsibility of fame and success
Different approaches to civil rights activism
The cost of speaking truth to power

actBreaks.description: After Clay's victory over Liston, the four friends gather at the Hampton House Motel, transitioning from individual backstories to their collective night together.

validation.ruling: Sam arrives at the Hampton House parking lot after Cassius's victory, marking the irreversible shift from the fight to the intimate character study that will dominate the rest of the film, fundamentally changing the story's direction from public spectacle to private revelation.

actBreaks.description: The friends move to the rooftop where their philosophical debates intensify and personal conflicts begin to surface more dramatically.

validation.ruling: The four men move to the rooftop where Malcolm reveals his growing paranoia about surveillance and his decision to document his life story, marking the point where their celebration transforms into deeper confrontations about identity, loyalty, and survival that will drive the final act.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a chamber piece masquerading as a biographical drama. Powers uses a deceptively simple structure - four men in a room talking - but layers it with flashbacks and mounting psychological pressure. The script operates like a pressure cooker, with each act tightening the screws on these friendships until they nearly break. The genius lies in how Powers uses the confined space to force these larger-than-life figures to confront not just each other, but their own contradictions and fears about their place in history.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing deliberately slows after the boxing victory, creating an intimate, almost theatrical rhythm. Long dialogue sequences in scenes 23-35 build tension through conversation rather than action. The rooftop sequence (scene 36) serves as a pressure release valve before the final act's emotional climax.

incitingIncident.description: Cassius dominates Sonny Liston in the boxing ring, landing a flurry of punches while taunting him as Liston stumbles and looks to the crowd for help.

validation.ruling: Cassius defeats Sonny Liston in the boxing ring, shattering expectations and establishing his new status as heavyweight champion, which forces him into the spotlight and sets up the main conflicts around his identity, fame, and conversion to Islam.

midpoint.description: Malcolm celebrates Cassius's victory with Betty over the phone, confirming that Cassius will announce his conversion to Islam the next morning.

validation.ruling: Position sanity: original at 57.8%. Corrected to nearest turning-point in 35-72% window.

climax.description: Cassius announces his conversion to Islam to a crowd of reporters at the Hampton House pool, with Malcolm standing beside him as onlookers gather.

validation.ruling: Scene #43 is a flashback story, not the climactic confrontation. Scene #45 serves as the climax where Cassius publicly announces his conversion to Islam to reporters at the Hampton House pool with Malcolm beside him, representing the ultimate moment where he applies everything he's learned and makes his defining choice about his identity.

scenes.heading: INT. WEMBLEY STADIUM BOXING RING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cassius Clay dominates Henry Cooper in the boxing ring at Wembley Stadium, mocking his opponent while Angelo Dundee criticizes Clay's showboating approach to the fight.

scenes.heading: INT. COPACABANA NIGHTCLUB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sam Cooke watches comedian Myron Cohen perform to an all-white audience at the Copacabana, expressing displeasure at having to follow the popular act.

scenes.heading: INT. DRESSING ROOM - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sam explodes at Jess in his dressing room after being told he bombed, aggressively confronting him about his success before physically pushing him out.

scenes.heading: INT. 1960S CADILLAC - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Jim Brown drives his Cadillac down an unpaved Southern road and stops at a remote house.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. SIMONS ISLAND, GEORGIA - DAY
scenes.summary: Jim Brown visits Mr. Carlton's house in Georgia where Emily Carlton excitedly recognizes him as an NFL player and welcomes him warmly along with her grandfather.

scenes.heading: INT. TELEVISION STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Mike Wallace introduces a television documentary about Negro dissenters preaching hatred against whites in various public venues.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - QUEENS, NEW YORK CITY - EVENING
scenes.summary: Betty Shabazz watches the Mike Wallace documentary at home as Malcolm X appears on television introducing Elijah Muhammad at a Nation of Islam rally.

scenes.heading: INT. POOL -DAY
scenes.summary: Cassius Clay poses underwater in his boxing trunks while a photographer takes pictures of him throwing slow-motion punches.

scenes.heading: EXT. POOLSIDE
scenes.summary: Cassius emerges from the pool laughing while his team worries about his focus, and he dismisses concerns about Sonny Liston by comparing him to a circus bear.

scenes.heading: INT. FONTAINEBLEAU HOTEL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Sam and Barbara Cooke work together on a song in their hotel room, with Sam struggling to write lyrics that fit his brother L.C.'s voice and style.

scenes.heading: INT. MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Malcolm calls Sam on the phone while Barbara disappointedly returns to the bathroom, and Malcolm asks about Cassius's whereabouts before the fight.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE MOTEL
scenes.summary: Cassius walks through the Hampton House Hotel parking lot toward the building.

scenes.heading: INT. MALCOLM’S HOTEL ROOM
scenes.summary: Cassius arrives at Malcolm's hotel room where they embrace, and Malcolm retrieves prayer rugs while Cassius calls Malcolm his insurance policy.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL BALCONY, POOLSIDE
scenes.summary: Kareem and Jamaal conduct their prayer ritual while standing outside Malcolm's room on the hotel balcony.

scenes.heading: INT. MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Malcolm and Cassius complete their prayer and discuss fight strategy, with Malcolm suggesting Cassius tone down his rhetoric, leading to Cassius explaining his admiration for wrestler Gorgeous George's showmanship.

scenes.heading: INT. MIAMI CONVENTION CENTER - FIGHT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene transition to the Miami Convention Center on fight night.

scenes.heading: INT. BOXING RING
scenes.summary: Cassius dominates Sonny Liston in the boxing ring, landing a flurry of punches while taunting him as Liston stumbles and looks to the crowd for help.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cassius defeats the heavily favored Sonny Liston to become heavyweight champion, fundamentally changing his status and setting up the central conflicts of the film.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE MOTEL PARKING LOT - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sam arrives at the Hampton House parking lot in his car where Kareem greets him and confirms he's the first to arrive at the victory party.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE MOTEL - POOLSIDE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Sam and Kareem walk past the hotel pool and up the stairs where they meet Jamaal, another Nation of Islam guard who appears starstruck by Sam.

scenes.heading: INT. MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Sam enters Malcolm's empty room and examines the modest surroundings with disappointment, calling it a dump as an airplane passes overhead causing the room to shake.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE PARKING LOT
scenes.summary: Malcolm, Jim, Cassius, and Jerome arrive at the Hampton House in a Lincoln as Kareem greets them in the parking lot.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE MOTEL
scenes.summary: The group heads upstairs to Malcolm's room as Kareem informs Malcolm that Sam has already arrived, and Malcolm dismisses the guards for privacy.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE MOTEL - MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: The four friends celebrate Cassius's victory in Malcolm's room, with Cassius energetically recounting the fight while the others joke and banter, until tension builds when Sam challenges Malcolm's authority and religious beliefs.
scenes.turningPointNote: The celebration turns into the first major ideological confrontation between Sam and Malcolm, establishing the central conflict about identity, religion, and activism that will drive the rest of the film.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTSIDE HOTEL ROOM DOOR
scenes.summary: Malcolm exits the room to get something from the car while Sam sarcastically questions his security concerns, and Jamaal remains posted at the door.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Jim lies on Malcolm's bed trying to ignore Cassius jumping on it while Sam complains about staying in the small room all night.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE - PARKING LOT
scenes.summary: Malcolm retrieves a camera from the car and tells Kareem and Jerome he wants to make a private phone call to his wife from the telephone booth.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Jim discovers Sam's hidden flask of alcohol in his guitar case despite Sam's protests, while Jim holds a bowl of ice cream.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE - TELEPHONE BOOTH
scenes.summary: Malcolm calls home and speaks with his young daughter Attalah from a telephone booth outside the hotel.

scenes.heading: INT. MALCOLM’S HOUSE - QUEENS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Malcolm guides his daughter Attalah through finding a book and reading a note he left for the family, creating a tender moment between father and daughter.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Betty takes the phone from Attalah and sends her to bed, while in Malcolm's room Jim offers Cassius the flask just as Jamaal knocks and enters asking for an autograph.

scenes.heading: INT. TELEPHONE BOOTH
scenes.summary: Malcolm celebrates Cassius's victory with Betty over the phone, confirming that Cassius will announce his conversion to Islam the next morning.
scenes.turningPointNote: Malcolm reveals his plan for Cassius to publicly convert to Islam, showing the strategic nature of their relationship and setting up the religious transformation that will define Muhammad Ali.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Cassius admires himself in the mirror and drinks from the flask while Jim confides about starring in a western film as a Buffalo Soldier tracking a Confederate general.

scenes.heading: EXT. TELEPHONE BOOTH
scenes.summary: Malcolm continues his phone conversation with Betty while noticing two white men in dress shirts watching him from across the street.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOTEL - PARKING LOT/TELEPHONE BOOTH
scenes.summary: Sam approaches Malcolm at the telephone booth after finishing a cigarette and being recognized by a passing woman, playfully teasing Malcolm about checking in with his wife.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM'S ROOM
scenes.summary: Jim hears voices outside and warns Cassius to keep quiet about the movie discussion as Malcolm and Sam return to the room, with Malcolm appearing nervous and looking out the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE - ROOFTOP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The four men go up to the hotel rooftop where Malcolm expresses paranoia about being watched, leading to a major confrontation where Sam challenges Malcolm's beliefs and authority while Malcolm defends the Nation of Islam's mission.
scenes.turningPointNote: The rooftop confrontation escalates the ideological conflict, with Sam directly challenging Malcolm's commitment to the Nation of Islam and questioning whether he truly believes in what he preaches.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE - OUTER CORRIDOR - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The group walks back to Malcolm's room trailing behind Kareem, with Jim criticizing Malcolm's security people and Sam making disparaging comments.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM'S ROOM
scenes.summary: Back in the room, Malcolm lectures the others about the threats they all face as successful Black men, arguing that their achievements make them targets for harassment and FBI surveillance.

scenes.heading: INT. BATHROOM
scenes.summary: Jim listens from the bathroom as Malcolm continues his speech about their roles in the struggle, then returns as Sam directly challenges Malcolm about following Elijah Muhammad's orders and questions Malcolm's true commitment to the Nation.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sam's direct confrontation about Malcolm's relationship with Elijah Muhammad forces Malcolm to reveal the complexity of his position within the Nation, foreshadowing his eventual break from the organization.

scenes.heading: EXT. LIQUOR STORE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cassius and Sam exit a liquor store and encounter two kids admiring Sam's Ferrari, with Sam giving them money before they congratulate Cassius on his victory.

scenes.heading: INT. SAM’S CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: In Sam's car, Cassius confirms his conversion to Islam and explains his philosophy about young, Black, famous, and unapologetic men needing to support each other, while Sam warns him about the target on his back.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM’S ROOM
scenes.summary: Cassius and Sam return to the room with whiskey, but Malcolm expresses disappointment upon learning that Cassius has been drinking alcohol despite his religious conversion.

scenes.heading: INT. BOSTON BALLROOM - NIGHT - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: Malcolm tells a story about seeing Sam perform after Jackie Wilson at a Boston ballroom, describing how Sam captivated the audience with his sensual performance style.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - MALCOLM'S ROOM
scenes.summary: Malcolm completes his story and praises Sam's talent, but then reveals his complicated relationship with the Nation of Islam and uncertainty about his future with the organization, shocking Cassius.
scenes.turningPointNote: Malcolm reveals he may be leaving the Nation of Islam, fundamentally altering Cassius's understanding of his mentor and the religious path he's chosen to follow.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAMPTON HOUSE HOTEL - POOL
scenes.summary: Cassius announces his conversion to Islam to a crowd of reporters at the Hampton House pool, with Malcolm standing beside him as onlookers gather.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cassius publicly declares his conversion to Islam, fulfilling Malcolm's plan and marking his transformation from Cassius Clay to the man who will become Muhammad Ali.

scenes.heading: INT. HAMPTON HOUSE - DINER - LATER
scenes.summary: In the hotel diner, Cassius poses for photos with Malcolm while wearing a Muslim suit and bowtie, celebrating with the crowd as Sam Cooke's 'Good Times' plays.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TONIGHT SHOW - NIGHT - WEEKS LATER
scenes.summary: Sam performs 'Good Times' on The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson and Ed McMahon, discussing his songwriting process and philosophy about observing people and current events.

scenes.heading: INT. NATION OF ISLAM MOSQUE - DAY
scenes.summary: Elijah Muhammad introduces Cassius Clay to a Nation of Islam crowd as Muhammad Ali while Sam's voice sings 'A Change Is Gonna Come' in voiceover.

scenes.heading: EXT. MALCOLM'S HOUSE - QUEENS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. DIRTY DOZEN SET - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. MOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Malcolm sits in contemplation with his sleeping daughter Attalah on his lap while his other daughters and Betty sleep nearby, then looks at his completed autobiography manuscript and taps its cover with relief, knowing his legacy will survive.

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
